forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KRRE2K6TTREE37RJEARGRN9K
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8480'
+project: plugin-arch
+title: 'operations-macros: operation-tool macro that auto-attaches _meta'
+---
+## What
+Add macro support to `swissarmyhammer-operations-macros` so an operation tool declares itself from its operation set and the `io.swissarmyhammer/operations` `_meta` is attached to the tool's `Tool` definition automatically — operation tools become self-describing by construction and `_meta` can never drift from the operation structs.
+
+- In `crates/swissarmyhammer-operations-macros/src/lib.rs`, add the operation-tool analogue of `#[operation]` (e.g. an attribute macro or `operation_tool!` decl macro — pick whichever fits the existing `#[operation]`/`#[param]` style; the `#[operation]` macro is the pattern to mirror).
+- The macro takes a tool name, description, and the operation set, and emits code that builds the tool's `Tool` definition with both: the flat `inputSchema` from `generate_mcp_schema` AND `_meta["io.swissarmyhammer/operations"]` from `generate_operations_meta` (the generator added in the operations crate).
+- A tool author writes the operation structs + the macro invocation; they never hand-assemble `_meta`.
+- Wire format unchanged — `op` stays the single selector; the `tools/call` handler stays an `op` match.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] A new macro exists in `swissarmyhammer-operations-macros`, documented with a usage example mirroring the `#[operation]` doc style.
+- [ ] Code generated by the macro produces a Tool whose `_meta` carries `io.swissarmyhammer/operations` with the noun→verb→parameters tree, and whose `inputSchema` is the existing flat `op`-enum schema.
+- [ ] `_meta` content is byte-identical to `generate_operations_meta` over the same operation slice (no second source of truth).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] A `trybuild`-style or in-crate compile + runtime test: declare a small tool with two operations via the macro, assert the resulting Tool definition has `_meta["io.swissarmyhammer/operations"]["<noun>"]["<verb>"].op` set correctly and `inputSchema.properties.op.enum` lists both ops.
+- [ ] Test that a tool with no `_meta` operations key is treated as flat (negative control — a flat tool is not mis-tagged).
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-operations-macros` — all green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the macro-output assertions first, then implement the macro.
+
+## Depends on
+generate_operations_meta from the operations crate (sibling task).
\ No newline at end of file
